text.title: Prometheus Monitoring: Core Concepts and Metric Types
text.summary: Prometheus pull model, metric types, label best practices, and CI/CD tool integration matrix.

section.title: Metric Types
section.p: Counter: monotonically increasing value; reset on restart. Use for request count, error count, build count.
Gauge: value that goes up and down. Use for queue size, active connections, temperature, memory usage.
Histogram: samples observations in configurable buckets; exposes _count, _sum, _bucket. Use for request duration, build time. Enables quantile calculation via histogram_quantile().
Summary: pre-calculates quantiles client-side over a sliding window. Use only when histogram bucket boundaries cannot be predicted; histograms are preferred.

rule.statement: Never use unbounded label values (user_id, request_id, UUID) — each unique label value creates a new time series and can exhaust Prometheus memory.
rule.rationale: A metric with 1M unique user_id values creates 1M time series. Prometheus stores all active time series in memory. 10k time series is fine; 10M crashes Prometheus.

rule.statement: Use consistent label names across all services: `namespace`, `service`, `pod`, `env`. Document the label schema.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent labels prevent cross-service aggregation in dashboards and alerts. Standardizing on Kubernetes metadata labels (from relabeling) ensures consistency automatically.

section.title: CI/CD Tool Integration
section.p: Jenkins: install Prometheus Metrics Plugin; scrape `/prometheus` endpoint.
GitLab CI: deploy `gitlab-ci-pipelines-exporter`; configure with GitLab API token.
GitHub Actions: no native endpoint; push metrics to Pushgateway after each workflow run.
ArgoCD: native Prometheus metrics on port 8082; use ServiceMonitor.
Tekton: built-in metrics endpoint on port 9090.

rule.statement: Use Pushgateway only for short-lived jobs (CI/CD pipelines, batch jobs) that cannot be scraped; never use it as a general metrics proxy for long-running services.
rule.rationale: Long-running services should expose a `/metrics` endpoint for Prometheus to scrape. Pushgateway holds the last pushed value indefinitely — a failed long-running service would still show its last metrics as if healthy.
